title: Lost Connections
subtitle: Uncovering the Real Causes of Depression — and the Unexpected Solutions
author: Johann Hari
themes: Depression and anxiety are largely social and environmental, not purely biochemical
The serotonin hypothesis is weakly supported by evidence
Disconnection — from meaning, community, nature, work — drives mental illness
Reconnection is the path to recovery: social prescribing, meaningful work, community
Critique of the biomedical/pharmaceutical model of mental health treatment
excerpt: I was told, for years, that my depression was caused by a chemical imbalance in my brain. I was told this was the plain, scientific truth. But when I started to investigate the evidence, I found something astonishing: this story is not true. The chemical-imbalance theory of depression has been shown to be false by the scientific community for decades. So why are we still being told this story? And what, if not a chemical imbalance, is causing the depression and anxiety that so many of us experience?
